title: SDIFF
description: Returns the difference of multiple sets.
complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of elements in all given sets.

returns.description: A set with members of the resulting set
Long count of members of the resulting set.

nredisstack_async.signature: SetCombine(SetOperation operation, RedisKey[] keys, CommandFlags flags = CommandFlags.None)

params.description: keys pointing to sets to subtract
params.name: *keys

content:
Returns the members of the set resulting from the difference between the first set and all the successive sets. For example: ``` key1 = {a,b,c,d} key2 = {c} key3 = {a,c,e} SDIFF key1 key2 key3 = {b,d} ``` Keys that do not exist are considered to be empty sets. ## Examples {{% redis-cli %}} SADD key1 "a" SADD key1 "b" SADD key1 "c" SADD key2 "c" SADD key2 "d" SADD key2 "e" SDIFF key1 key2 {{% /redis-cli %}} ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="sdiff-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list with members of the resulting set. -tab-sep- [Set reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#sets): a set with the members of the resulting set. {{< /multitabs >}}
example:
SADD key1 "a"
SADD key1 "b"
SADD key1 "c"
SADD key2 "c"
SADD key2 "d"
SADD key2 "e"
SDIFF key1 key2
notes: This command's behavior varies in clustered Redis environments. See the [multi-key operations]() page for more information.

sections.title: Overview
Examples
sections.content:
{{% redis-cli %}}
SADD key1 "a"
SADD key1 "b"
SADD key1 "c"
SADD key2 "c"
SADD key2 "d"
SADD key2 "e"
SDIFF key1 key2
{{% /redis-cli %}}
